title: Stellar.org official site search (Algolia pages index, English)
description: Intent-named search operations over the official stellar.org site — the Stellar Development Foundation's own pages: foundation/mandate/structure, Enterprise Fund, grants and funding, quarterly reports, Foundation News and press releases, official use cases and case studies, products and events. Authored spec-as-data mirroring specs/stellar-docs.json: each operation declares {id, description, params} for the unified catalog plus an `algolia` block recording the exact query mapping for the execute adapter. English only by FIXED INDEX SELECTION: the site is multi-language via separate per-locale indices (English `pages`, Spanish `pages_es` with translated slugs); the `language` record field is NOT facetable (facetFilters/filters on it return zero hits), so the English mechanism is the host-owned index choice, never a filter and never a caller argument. Authority boundary: primary source for SDF organization, mandate, funding/transparency, official announcements/press, and official adoption/use-case claims — NOT protocol mechanics, SDK/API reference, current on-chain values, or ecosystem completeness (use stellarDocs / scout / lumenloop for those).
authoredBy: todo 899 (design spike in todo comment 2520; index/language research + live census in Solo scratchpad 609 R1: separate pages/pages_es indices, language not facetable, SITE key reaches list+settings)

backend.retry: network/5xx only, escalating timeout, fallback hosts in order; never retry 4xx; treat 429 as back-off, not host-retry

constraints.englishOnlyMechanism: Index selection IS the language mechanism. Production locales are separate primary indices: `pages` (English, 5,577 records at authoring) and `pages_es` (Spanish, 5,485, translated root slugs like /fondo-empresarial — no /es/ prefix exists). `language` is absent from attributesForFaceting: a correctly formed facetFilters:["language:en"] or filters:"language:en" request live-returned 0 hits for a 176-hit query. Never add a language facet, filter, or URL-locale heuristic; never expose index choice to callers. If a future inventory snapshot shows `pages` becoming mixed-language, FAIL the language gate and require a newly measured design.
constraints.recordHeterogeneity: Records are page- and section-grain across _type values (blog 550, event ~187, press 106, foundation 38, funnel, learn, case study, resource, collection, products, use case, home in the accessible sample). Field sets vary by _type (blogs add date/author/blogType/tags; events add schedule; sections add body/parent). The adapter guarantees a stable normalized subset and must never coerce a nested object to '[object Object]'.
constraints.urlNotFilterable: URL is not a facet; path-family operations over-fetch and filter client-side by url prefix (same clientFilter pattern as stellarDocs categories). Path filters select CONTENT families only — they are not and must never be a language mechanism.
constraints.distinct: The index default is distinct:true on url; operations pin distinct:false so section-grain records remain visible, then client-side prefix filtering and truncation bound the response.
constraints.authorityBoundary: Official SDF copy is primary for organization/mandate/funding/transparency statements and official announcements; it is marketing-adjacent for everything else. Not authority for protocol mechanics, API/SDK reference (stellarDocs), current on-chain or market values, third-party operational status, or unqualified current financial totals — figures on press/announcement pages are as-of their publication date.
constraints.zeroHitSemantics: Zero hits means the official site does not cover the topic — treat as soft-empty (inconclusive for the ecosystem at large; other services may still answer).

catalogHints.note: Each operation maps 1:1 to a CatalogEntry — id/description/params verbatim; transport from backend + operation.algolia.

operations.name: search_official_site
operations.description: Keyword search across the ENTIRE official stellar.org site (English) — the Stellar Development Foundation's own pages: foundation and mandate, Enterprise Fund, grants and funding, quarterly reports, Foundation News, press releases, use cases, case studies, products, learning pages, and events. Use when an official SDF statement, announcement, program page, or event is the ground truth wanted and no narrower stellarOrg operation fits. Results are official first-party copy: primary for what SDF says and runs, NOT for protocol mechanics or API reference (use the stellarDocs operations) and NOT for current on-chain or market values. Zero hits = the official site does not cover it; other sources may.

query.description: Keyword query over official site pages.

operations.returns: Array of hits: { title, url (site-relative, e.g. /foundation/mandate), type (_type: blogPage | pressReleasePage | foundationPage | caseStudyPage | useCasePage | eventPage | ...), description?, date?, author?, blogType?, tags? } plus { nbHits }.

verified.note: same query against pages_es returns 15 Spanish hits topped by /fondo-empresarial/portafolio — index selection verified as the language boundary

operations.name: search_foundation_org
operations.description: Search the Stellar Development Foundation's ORGANIZATION pages: /foundation (structure, team, the mandate and its dated revisions — nonprofit, no shareholders, entrusted lumens), /enterprise-fund (the SDF Enterprise Fund program, portfolio, and investment announcements), /grants-and-funding (matching fund, academic research grants, bug bounty and other funding programs), and /quarterly-reports (SDF transparency reports). The primary source for what SDF is, how it is structured and governed, what its mandate says, and what it funds. Figures are as-of their page or report date — cite the URL and date rather than asserting current totals. Not for community/ecosystem grant recipients' own claims (scout/lumenloop) or protocol documentation (stellarDocs).

hitsPerPage.description: Post-filter hits returned; the adapter over-fetches then keeps organization-family URLs.

operations.returns: Array of organization-family hits: { title, url, type, description?, date? } plus { rawNbHits, filteredCount }.

clientFilter.why: URL is not a facet — the adapter over-fetches (hitsPerPage 50) and keeps hits whose site-relative url starts with an organization-family prefix, then truncates to the requested hitsPerPage. Content-family selection only; language is handled by the fixed `pages` index.

also.query: Stellar Development Foundation nonprofit structure mandate no shareholders

operations.name: search_foundation_news_press
operations.description: Search official SDF ANNOUNCEMENTS: Foundation News blog posts (/blog/foundation-news/ — program launches, mandate updates, safety and anti-scam guidance like the official 'protect yourself from scammers' guide, partnership and milestone announcements) and press releases (/press/ — investments, quarterly review releases, event announcements). The primary source for what SDF has officially announced and when; every hit carries its publication date — cite it, since announcement figures age. Not for third-party news coverage or community commentary (lumenloop carries editorial/ecosystem content) and not for developer changelogs (stellarDocs).

hitsPerPage.description: Post-filter hits returned; the adapter over-fetches then keeps news/press URLs.

operations.returns: Array of announcement hits: { title, url, type, description?, date?, author?, blogType? } plus { rawNbHits, filteredCount }.

clientFilter.why: URL is not a facet — over-fetch then keep news/press-family URLs, truncate to hitsPerPage. Content-family selection only.

verified.query: how to protect yourself from scammers Stellar

operations.name: search_use_cases_case_studies
operations.description: Search official Stellar USE CASES and CASE STUDIES: /use-cases/ (payments, asset tokenization, remittances and other solution pages), /case-studies/ (named real-world deployments — MoneyGram, UNHCR and other institutional adopters, with what was built and why), and /products-and-tools/ (official product pages). The primary source for SDF's own account of who uses Stellar in production and for which institutional flows. First-party adoption narrative: pair with scout (live ecosystem/project data) or lumenloop (independent editorial coverage) when corroboration or current status is needed. Not for protocol how-tos (stellarDocs).

hitsPerPage.description: Post-filter hits returned; the adapter over-fetches then keeps use-case/case-study/product URLs.

operations.returns: Array of adoption-family hits: { title, url, type, description?, tags? } plus { rawNbHits, filteredCount }.

clientFilter.why: URL is not a facet — over-fetch then keep adoption-family URLs, truncate to hitsPerPage. Content-family selection only.

verified.note: raw top-1 is the broad /fintech-solutions marketing page; the family filter puts the case study first — the reason this operation exists
